# Termicolor

> Generate terminal color schemes from any image

## Overview

Termicolor is a free web application that extracts dominant colors from images and generates complete terminal color palettes. It's designed for developers who want to create custom, visually cohesive terminal themes.

Website: https://termicolor.io

## What It Does

1. **Color Extraction**: Upload any image (JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF) and Termicolor extracts the dominant colors using advanced color quantization
2. **ANSI Mapping**: Automatically maps extracted colors to the 16 standard ANSI terminal colors (8 normal + 8 bright variants)
3. **Live Preview**: See your theme in a realistic terminal preview with syntax highlighting examples
4. **Contrast Analysis**: WCAG accessibility analysis ensures text remains readable
5. **Multi-Format Export**: Download configurations for all major terminals

## Supported Export Formats

- iTerm2 (.itermcolors XML)
- Alacritty (YAML)
- Kitty (conf)
- Windows Terminal (JSON)
- Hyper (JavaScript)
- Terminal.app (macOS)

## How to Use

1. Visit https://termicolor.io
2. Upload an image by drag-and-drop or file picker
3. View the extracted color palette
4. Adjust individual color mappings if needed (click any color swatch)
5. Toggle between dark/light mode preview
6. Run contrast analysis to check accessibility
7. Export to your preferred terminal format

## URL Sharing

Color schemes can be shared via URL. The `?c=` parameter contains a base64-encoded color scheme:

Example: `https://termicolor.io/?c=<encoded-colors>`

When someone visits a shared URL, the color scheme is automatically loaded.

## Technical Details

- Client-side only (no server processing of images)
- No data collection or tracking
- Works offline after initial load
- Mobile-responsive design

## Related Topics

- Terminal customization
- ANSI color codes
- iTerm2 theming
- Developer tools
- Color theory
- Accessibility (WCAG contrast)

## Contact

Website: https://termicolor.io

For extended documentation, see: https://termicolor.io/llms-full.txt
